idle.description: Try the short tutorial first for a dry run, or go straight to configuring your session—name, direction mode, trial count—and launch the fullscreen protocol.
idle.title: Motor Imagery
idle.tutorialDoneSubtitle: Great — you're ready to start the real session.

session.note: The session will be recorded in the system. After confirmation, the Motor Imagery protocol will start in fullscreen mode.
session.title: New EEG session

tutorial.leftDesc: Imagine reaching with your left hand to the left — as if grabbing something.
tutorial.notSaved: The trial session is {strong} in the system. You can repeat it as many times as you like before the real session.
tutorial.rightDesc: Imagine reaching with your right hand to the right — as if grabbing something.
tutorial.step1Body: Motor Imagery is a mental technique that involves imagining performing a movement — without actually moving. Your brain generates characteristic electrical patterns that NeuraFlow records and processes.
tutorial.step2Body: When the arrow appears, do not move — {strong} in the given direction. Keep that thought for as long as the arrow is visible.
tutorial.step3Body: You will perform {strong}: 2 × left and 2 × right in a fixed order. During the session a hint will appear on screen reminding you what to imagine.
tutorial.trialFlowTitle: What does a single trial look like?

models.dataForModelSubtitle: Completed EEG sessions you can use as training data. Record new sessions on the Sessions page.
models.dataForModelSubtitlePicking: Tick the sessions to include, then tap Train model.
models.subtitle: Trained machine learning models.
models.title: Models

sessions.calibrationSubtitle: Create a new session, choose direction mode and trials per direction, then start fullscreen Motor Imagery recording.
sessions.choiceSubtitle: Select the type of brain-computer interface session you want to record.
sessions.focusSubtitle: Record EEG sessions for focus and relaxation states. Train models to detect your mental state in real time.
sessions.motorImageryNewSessionSubtitle: Choose direction mode and number of trials, then start the fullscreen Motor Imagery protocol.
sessions.motorImageryPageSubtitle: Record brain signal data while imagining directional movements. Each session adds labeled EEG data for training your model.
sessions.motorImagerySubtitle: Train your BCI model by imagining hand and body movements. The system detects directional intent from EEG signals.
sessions.noActiveSession: No active EEG session. Start a new one to begin recording.
sessions.protocolStep1Desc: Put on the EEG cap and make sure all electrode impedances are optimal before starting.
sessions.protocolStep2Desc: Choose a session name, direction mode (2 or 4-way) and number of trials per direction.
sessions.protocolStep3Desc: A fixation cross appears, then an arrow cue. Imagine the movement without moving. Repeat for all trials.
sessions.protocolStep4Desc: When finished, the session is saved and appears in the history below. Use it to train your model.
sessions.subtitle: Manage EEG recording sessions for machine learning.
sessions.title: EEG Sessions
